rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that reference component ports, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent-level bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE contracts, avoiding package-qualified names not present in the analyzed model.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for feature-based assumptions/guarantees, implementations for subcomponent bindings.
Generated AGREE code must reference only identifiers and types present in the input AADL model to maintain scope consistency.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks, with proper use of logical operators and initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations.
Component type contracts should avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths; use visible ports and features only.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees or assignments in component type annexes unless they are valid bindings to visible outputs.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model into AGREE annexes.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
